Sony says all new PlayStation games from both first- and third-party developers will be sold in digital formats from January 2028, ending physical game discs  —  The end of an era, a potential fatal blow to video games as physical media, and a hint of what's in store for the PS6?

items.title: Regularly $700 Cooler Master Sneaker X PC case with 360mm liquid cooler and 850W PSU just plunged 68% to $225 all-time low
items.summary: Over at Woot, as part of its wider PC components sale, you can now score the updated Cooler Master Sneaker X PC case for $224.99 Prime shipped (a $6 delivery fee will apply otherwise), with a 20% discount that auto-applies during checkout. This is the latest configuration of the case, complete with Cooler Master’s pre-installed 360mm Flux liquid cooler and an 850W V850 SFX Gold ATX 3.0 power supply. It typically sells for around $700 at Amazon, and while Woot has discounted it a few times in recent months, today’s offer marks the lowest price we have tracked yet at roughly 68% off. It is still a lot pricier than a typical PC case, but the included liquid cooler and 850W PSU make this a particularly notable value at $224.99. Head below for more details.

items.title: Sony is killing all physical PlayStation game discs
items.summary: Sony has announced that from January 2028 it will entirely stop the production of physical discs for new PlayStation games. From that point onwards, new releases will only be available in digital versions from the PlayStation Store and other retailers. Games released before January 2028 will still be available on disc. "This is a natural […]

items.title: Walmart has one of the best RTX 5070 gaming PC deals right now with this CyberPowerPC desktop down at $1,349
items.summary: Walmart has been rolling out some great gaming PC deals lately, and this CyberPowerPC configuration is another one of those at $1,349 shipped. That’s $521 off its usual $1,870 price tag and one of the best prices we have tracked so far for a desktop packing NVIDIA’s RTX 5070 graphics card. Alongside the GPU, you’re getting a liquid-cooled, unlocked Intel Core Ultra 5 processor for strong overall gaming performance. Comparable RTX 5070 desktops are still generally selling closer to $2,000 at other retailers, making this a compelling option for anyone looking to build a capable 1440p gaming setup without overspending.

items.title: This compact 70W charger has enough ports for a laptop, smartphone, and more, and it’s just $25 today
items.summary:
Baseus Enerfill 70W 3-port USB-C charger

Buy for $25 (Reg. $40)

What we love

Baseus’ Enerfill 70W charger is a compact travel-friendly option capable of powering a laptop, phone, and smaller accessories from one wall outlet. It features two USB-C ports and a USB-A port, and it can deliver enough power for a MacBook Air or similarly-specced notebook. Its foldable prongs also help keep the charger compact and easier to slip into a bag.

The sale includes displays from Samsung’s Odyssey lineup, LG UltraGear, AOC, and other brands we typically feature a lot around here, and you can save up to $700 on select models. Whether you are after a fast OLED for competitive gaming, a high-refresh-rate QHD panel, or a large curved screen for a more immersive setup, there are plenty of solid options to choose from.
